steps.title: Create two values of the function
steps.content: Since $g(9)=11$, one point on the line is $(9,11)$. The statement $g(x+1)-g(x)=2$ means the output increases by $2$ whenever $x$ increases by $1$, so $g(10)=13$.

steps.title: Find the line from the data
steps.content: The output changes by $2$ for every $1$ increase in $x$, so the slope is $m=2$. Use $g(9)=11$ in $g(x)=2x+b$: $$11=2(9)+b$$ $$b=-7$$ Therefore $$g(x)=2x-7$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice D matches $g(x)=2x-7$. It also checks directly: $g(9)=2(9)-7=11$, so choice D is correct.
